data.title: Darren Chan Maintains Buy Rating on CapitaLand Investment with Unchanged S$3.69 Price Target, Citing Resilient Fee-Based Growth and Asset-Light Strategy
data.description:
CapitaLand Investment Limited, the Real Estate sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Darren Chan from Phillip Securities maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a S$3.69 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Darren Chan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to CapitaLand Investment’s resilient fee-based growth and asset-light strategy. He highlights that fee-related revenue grew solidly year over year, supported by strong performance in both listed and private funds management, and expects this momentum to continue as more event-driven fees are recognised from upcoming transactions.

At the same time, he points to robust capital-raising and deployment activity year to date, as well as a secured mandate that should significantly boost recurring private fund management fees. Despite acknowledging headwinds in China and a more uncertain macroeconomic backdrop, he believes the upcoming listing of a second C-REIT and continued capital recycling support earnings visibility, justifying a maintained Buy rating and an unchanged target price of S$3.69.

In another report released today, CGS International also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a S$4.19 price target.

data.title: Myles Minter Reiterates Buy on Alnylam After Strong Beat, Reaffirmed 2026 Outlook, and Advancing TRITON Phase III Pipeline
data.description:
William Blair analyst Myles Minter has maintained their bullish stance on ALNY stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Myles Minter has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Alnylam’s strong quarterly execution and reaffirmed long‑term outlook. The company delivered revenue and earnings that exceeded both his projections and Street expectations, driven largely by robust growth in the TTR franchise, particularly Amvuttra’s continued uptake in ATTR-CM, which supports confidence in the full-year revenue targets.

Minter also highlights management’s unchanged 2026 guidance, which points to multibillion-dollar product revenue and disciplined expense control, reinforcing a compelling growth and profitability trajectory. In addition, accelerated enrollment and expansion of the Phase III TRITON trial for nucresiran, while keeping the anticipated 2030 launch timeline intact, strengthens his conviction in the company’s late-stage pipeline and future value creation potential.

Minter covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Dianthus Therapeutics, Immunic, and Moderna. According to TipRanks, Minter has an average return of 34.6% and a 59.09% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released yesterday, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $510.00 price target.

data.title: Regeneron: Buy Rating Reiterated as Analyst Sees Melanoma Trial Sell-Off Overdone; $900 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
BMO Capital analyst Evan Seigerman maintained a Buy rating on Regeneron yesterday and set a price target of $900.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Evan Seigerman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including confidence in Regeneron’s core business and its ability to generate long-term value from research investments. He views the sell-off following protocol changes in the fianlimab/Libtayo melanoma trial as potentially overdone, arguing that slower event rates could still reflect meaningful efficacy rather than control-arm outperformance.

Seigerman also underscores a positive outlook on Regeneron’s oncology franchise and maintains cautious optimism about the earlier-stage pipeline, which together support continued enterprise value growth. In his view, Regeneron’s track record in converting R&D spend into tangible economic gains provides a solid foundation for an Outperform/Buy stance, with the unchanged price target set at $900.

Seigerman covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Merck & Company, Novo Nordisk, and Eli Lilly & Co. According to TipRanks, Seigerman has an average return of 7.3% and a 49.60% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $917.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Verisk Buy Rating Reaffirmed on AI Edge and Resilient Subscription Growth Despite Price Target Trim to $215
data.description:
Jeffrey Silber, an analyst from BMO Capital, reiterated the Buy rating on Verisk Analytics. The associated price target was lowered to $215.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jeffrey Silber has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Verisk’s ability to outperform earnings expectations while maintaining conservative full-year guidance. He notes that solid subscription growth, strong pricing, and resilience across key insurance-focused segments offset weaker transactional revenues tied to temporary headwinds such as lighter weather-related activity and a government contract pause.

Silber also underscores Verisk’s advantaged position in AI, where its proprietary, industry-wide datasets and embedded workflows create a differentiated, data-centric offering that is difficult for competitors to replicate. With management reaffirming its 2026 outlook, emphasizing that first-quarter results likely mark the low point for growth, and the company sustaining best-in-class margins supported by a near-monopoly in P&C risk assessment, he sees an attractive risk-reward profile despite trimming his price target to $215.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $275.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Brinker as Solid Results Meet Emerging Macro Headwinds and Limited Margin Visibility
data.description:
Brinker International, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Andrew Strelzik from BMO Capital maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $175.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Strelzik has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including solid but moderating performance and emerging headwinds. Brinker’s latest quarter slightly beat earnings expectations, and Chili’s continues to post mid-single-digit sales gains, aided by successful menu initiatives like the chicken sandwich, yet traffic and comparable sales are normalizing as macro pressures such as higher gas prices and softer discretionary spending begin to weigh on customers.

The company reaffirmed its near-term margin expansion outlook and lifted the low end of its earnings guidance, but rising food costs and plans for lower price increases constrain visibility into margin gains beyond the current fiscal year. While operational metrics, guest satisfaction, and a healthy pipeline of initiatives for fiscal 2027 support continued outperformance versus the industry, Strelzik sees a balanced risk‑reward profile at current valuation, leading him to maintain a Market Perform (Hold) stance rather than a more aggressive rating.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Embecta, Maintains $22 Price Target Amid Core Strength and Platform Expansion
data.description:
Analyst Marie Thibault from BTIG maintained a Buy rating on Embecta Corporation and keeping the price target at $22.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Marie Thibault has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight both stability and emerging growth drivers for Embecta. She views the company’s core diabetes business as resilient, with management consistently meeting financial objectives even while managing headwinds in China, U.S. pricing pressure, and channel-related volume shifts, and she expects international strength and a second-half recovery in China supported by a new value-focused pen needle.

Thibault also emphasizes Embecta’s evolving strategy to become a broader medical supplies platform, including GLP-1–related pen needle opportunities with more than 30 pharma partners and incremental upside from the pending Owen Mumford acquisition, which should expand manufacturing capabilities and third-party product distribution. In her view, the 2026 guidance framework, disciplined execution, and additional optionality from partnerships justify maintaining the $22 price target, which implies attractive value at roughly 7x her forward adjusted EPS estimate.
